pi_items.source: task.json description and verified in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh
task.json description and verified in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh

Task description explicitly states 'FOUR HORIZONTAL SWIMLANES labeled: House of Representatives, Senate, Conference Committee, President'. Verified against USA.gov and Congress.gov legislative process documentation.

Task description states 'at least 14 steps'. US federal legislative process includes: Introduction, Committee Referral, Hearings, Markup, Committee Vote, Floor Scheduling, Debate, Floor Vote in both chambers, Conference Committee reconciliation (when needed), Conference Report votes in both chambers, and Presidential Action. Total is well over 14 steps.

Task specifies DECISION POINTS for: (1) Vote Outcomes (Pass/Fail), (2) Versions Match? (Yes/No), (3) Presidential Action (Sign/Veto). Verified against USA.gov and Congress.gov legislative process documentation. Additional decisions exist for veto override.

Task explicitly requires 'A SECOND PAGE titled Veto Override showing the process if the President vetoes a bill'. Verified against Congress.gov Veto Override Procedure documentation (https://www.congress.gov/crs-product/RS22654) which details the distinct 2/3 majority requirement in both chambers.

All keywords verified in USA.gov, Congress.gov, and legislative process documentation: House of Representatives and Senate are the two chambers; President takes final action; Conference Committee reconciles differences; committee action is required in each chamber; voting occurs at multiple stages; veto power and veto override are constitutional powers; bill is the legislative vehicle; law is the end product.

privileged_info_summary: The US legislation swimlane task requires agents to create a cross-functional process diagram with four swimlanes (House of Representatives, Senate, Conference Committee, President) containing at least 14 process steps and 3 decision points, plus a second page documenting the veto override process. All metadata values are accurate: the expected files are legislation_process.drawio and legislation_process.png; the minimum requirements for swimlanes (4), steps (14), decisions (3), and pages (2) correctly reflect the complexity of the actual federal legislative process including committee referral, floor debate, voting in both chambers, conference committee reconciliation, presidential action, and veto override procedures. All required keywords (house, senate, president, conference, committee, vote, veto, bill, law) are essential domain terms in the legislative process.
